input_data.text_description: I provided a sketch. hand written, in 3d. it is clear. a simetrical design of a cabinet, empty spaces and all can be seen in the image. one thing to note is on top there is this steps design instead of a plaine line. Furthermore, the buldings that can be found on left and right have these two sides that are around 6cm (from left and right. the full building). this one that has a shape on the bottom and top on the cunt, you know hwat I am talking about. empty spaces and doors and all can be clearly seen in the image. the design should be light beiges painted wood.

images.quality_feedback: Score: 4.6/10 | Strengths: The drawing attempts to show the tripartite composition with a central section and two flanking side wings; Depth dimensions (450–500 mm base, 300–350 mm upper central) are annotated and broadly correct; The cornice projection dimension (60–120 mm) is noted and referenced; Anti-tip bracket notation (x6 total) is present and correctly specified; Materials legend is partially present at the bottom; The stepped depth condition between lower base and upper central section is indicated; Counter/bridge shelf depth transition (100–150 mm ledge) is dimensioned on the right side; Wall-fixing and stud coordination note is included | Issues: CRITICAL: This is NOT a floor plan. The image shows what appears to be a front elevation or a hybrid elevation/plan view — not a true top-down orthographic floor plan as specified. A floor plan should show the unit as seen from directly above, revealing depth, wall relationship, and footprint only.; The view type is fundamentally wrong — a floor plan would show rectangular outlines of the three cabinet modules in plan, with depth dimensions, wall relationship, and no elevation detail (no doors, no shelving labels in elevation format); The layout reads as a front elevation with some plan annotations overlaid, creating a confusing hybrid that is neither a proper floor plan nor a proper elevation; Severe text rendering errors throughout: 'inativing' instead of 'shelving', 'sitles' instead of 'stiles', 'pand foor' instead of 'panel door', 'doorsquare' instead of 'door', 'Richt cupboard' instead of 'Right cupboard', 'Cubbard' instead of 'Cupboard', 'Constr arotrire (conimant)' is illegible, 'Open abase' is meaningless, 'Certrial cornice (do.)' is garbled; The tripartite massing is not correctly shown — in the image the central section does not appear clearly taller than the side wings as required (stepped hierarchical roofline is absent in this view); The internal layout of the central section is incorrectly arranged: the upper zone shows 'Solid or glazed Doors' on the LEFT and 'Open shelving bay' on the RIGHT, but the specification requires: left open shelving bay | central door pair | right open shelving bay — the door pair should be CENTRAL, flanked by shelving bays on both sides; The right side wing upper zone label reads 'Open shelving (solid or glazed doors)' which is contradictory and incorrect — side wings have open shelving only, no doors in the upper zone; The lower zone of the central section is confusingly labelled with overlapping 'Center drawer bank', 'Center drawer', 'Drawer bank', 'Richt cupboard', 'Right cupboard' — the layout is not clearly resolved; The curved crown and base moldings on the side sections — a non-negotiable critical detail — are not visible or clearly shown in this view; The pilaster-like front stiles are labelled but not clearly differentiated or drawn with sufficient architectural clarity; The overall drawing quality is very low — linework is inconsistent, labels are poorly placed, and the drawing reads as an AI-generated approximation rather than a professional architectural floor plan; The title at the top reads 'Top-down orthographic pian' (typo: 'pian' instead of 'plan') which undermines professional credibility; No overall width dimension (3000–3400 mm) is clearly shown across the full unit; No clear indication of wall relationship — a floor plan must show the wall as a thick line at the rear of the unit; The depth of the side wings is not independently dimensioned in plan | Suggestions: Regenerate as a TRUE top-down orthographic floor plan: show the unit from directly above as a flat plan view with wall shown as a thick hatched line at the rear, cabinet footprint as rectangular outlines, depth dimensions front-to-back, and overall width dimension; In a correct floor plan, the three modules should appear as three adjacent rectangles of varying depth: side wings at 450–500 mm depth, lower central base at 450–500 mm depth, upper central section stepping back to 300–350 mm depth (shown as a dashed line or secondary outline); Show the stepped depth condition clearly in plan: the upper central section outline (dashed) set back 100–150 mm from the lower base outline (solid); Label each module clearly in plan: LEFT SIDE WING, CENTRAL SECTION, RIGHT SIDE WING with their respective width dimensions; Show the wall as a thick double line or hatched element at the rear of all three modules; Show anti-tip bracket positions as small symbols at the rear of each module against the wall; Show the cornice projection as a dashed outline extending forward of the cabinet face; All text must be clean, correctly spelled, and professionally formatted — use standard architectural lettering; Include a north arrow or orientation indicator, scale bar, and drawing title block; Dimension the overall width (3000–3400 mm), individual module widths, and depths clearly with proper dimension lines and arrowheads; Fix all internal layout errors: central section upper zone must show left shelving bay | central door pair | right shelving bay in plan; Remove all elevation-style content (door panel details, shelf lines in elevation) from the floor plan — these belong in elevation drawings

review_result.regeneration_instructions: CRITICAL CORRECTION REQUIRED: Generate a TRUE top-down orthographic floor plan (bird's-eye view looking straight down) of the tripartite library wall unit. This must NOT be an elevation or hybrid view. The floor plan must show: (1) A thick hatched double line representing the wall at the rear of the unit. (2) Three adjacent rectangular cabinet footprints in plan: LEFT SIDE WING (700–900 mm wide × 450–500 mm deep), CENTRAL SECTION (1400–1600 mm wide × 450–500 mm deep at base, with dashed outline at 300–350 mm depth showing the stepped-back upper section), RIGHT SIDE WING (700–900 mm wide × 450–500 mm deep). (3) The stepped depth of the upper central section shown as a dashed rectangle set back 100–150 mm from the front face of the lower base. (4) Cornice projection shown as a fine dashed line extending 60–120 mm forward of the cabinet front face. (5) Anti-tip wall bracket positions marked as small cross symbols at the rear of each module (2 per module, 6 total). (6) Clear dimension lines with arrowheads showing: overall width (3000–3400 mm), each module width, base depth (450–500 mm), upper central depth (300–350 mm), stepped ledge (100–150 mm), cornice projection (60–120 mm). (7) Module labels: LEFT SIDE WING, CENTRAL SECTION (LOWER BASE), CENTRAL SECTION (UPPER — STEPPED BACK), RIGHT SIDE WING. (8) A note: 'Upper central section steps back to 300–350 mm depth above counter shelf at 900 mm AFF — confirm with client'. (9) Wall-fixing note: 'Anti-tip brackets x6 total — coordinate with stud layout'. (10) Clean black linework on white background, no shading, no elevation detail, no door panel profiles, no shelf lines in elevation format. Professional architectural drafting standard. All text correctly spelled in clean sans-serif font. Include scale bar and drawing title: 'FLOOR PLAN — TRIPARTITE LIBRARY WALL UNIT'. Strict bilateral symmetry about vertical centreline must be evident in plan.

images.generation_prompt:
Generate a professional, high-quality architectural image based on the following design specification. The image must be precise, clean, and suitable for real-world construction or presentation purposes.

DESIGN SPECIFICATION:
Design: Traditional Tripartite Library Wall Unit — Classical Breakfront Cabinet
Type: furniture
Description: A large, formal, freestanding symmetrical tripartite display cabinet and bookcase wall unit executed in a classical/traditional architectural style. The composition

images.quality_feedback: Score: 7.2/10 | Strengths: Photorealistic render quality is excellent — lighting, shadows, and material textures are convincing and professionally presented; The tripartite breakfront massing is clearly established with a dominant central section flanked by two shorter side wings — the stepped hierarchical roofline reads correctly; Heritage cream/off-white painted finish is well-executed and period-appropriate throughout; The counter/bridge shelf with a contrasting natural wood top is visible and clearly marks the mid-height datum transition between upper and lower zones; The lower central drawer bank is present and correctly positioned between flanking cupboard doors; Upper central glazed door pair is present and correctly positioned between open shelving bays; Open shelving bays on both sides of the upper central section are populated with books and display objects, adding realism; Side wing lower cupboard doors are present and symmetrically placed; The continuous plinth base runs across all three sections, unifying the composition; The formal interior context (hardwood floor, coffered ceiling, period room) is appropriate and enhances the presentation; Antique brass/bronze hardware knobs are visible and period-appropriate; The depth transition at counter level appears to be represented, with the upper central section stepping back | Issues: CRITICAL: The central section crown is rendered as a full classical pediment (triangular broken pediment with acanthus finial) rather than a standard Georgian/Victorian classical cornice build-up (bed molding, fascia, crown). A pediment is an overly ornate and architecturally specific element not specified — it reads as a Baroque or Neoclassical secretaire rather than a Georgian breakfront bookcase cornice; CRITICAL: The side section crown moldings appear as heavy horizontal classical entablatures with dentil courses and pilaster capitals — these do not read as the specified curved decorative crown moldings (ogee, cove, cyma recta, or cyma reversa profiles). The side crowns should be graceful curved transitions, not full entablature treatments; CRITICAL: The side section base moldings do not appear to feature the specified curved decorative base molding profiles — the bases appear as simple flat plinths without the ogee/cove/cyma reversa curved profile that is described as a non-negotiable critical detail; The lower central drawer bank shows approximately 4 drawers in a single column rather than the specified 3 rows × 2 columns (approximately 6 drawers total) — the face subdivision is incorrect; The lower central flanking cupboard doors (left and right of the drawer bank) appear as flat recessed-panel doors rather than traditional raised-panel stile-and-rail construction — the panel profile reads as inset/flat rather than raised; The side wing upper shelving appears to have 3 shelves per side, which is acceptable, but the shelving bays on the side wings appear to be open on both sides (no back panel visible), which conflicts with the full structural back panel requirement; The pilaster-like front stiles at module junctions are present but are rendered as full Corinthian pilasters with carved capitals — this is more ornate than the specification's 'pilaster-like stiles' language implies, though it is not strictly incorrect for a high-end Georgian piece; The counter/bridge shelf top surface appears to be a natural wood finish (oak or similar) rather than painted to match the carcass — this may be acceptable as a two-tone scheme but requires client confirmation per the specification; The upper central section appears narrower relative to the side wings than the specification suggests (central section should be approximately 1400–1600 mm vs. side wings at 700–900 mm each) — the proportional width ratio appears compressed; The side wing height differential relative to the central section appears to be approximately correct at 75–80%, but the pediment on the central section artificially inflates the apparent height differential beyond what the carcass alone would show; Drawer pulls on the lower central bank appear as small round knobs rather than the specified bin pulls or cup pulls — while knobs are period-appropriate, bin pulls are the preferred specification | Suggestions: Replace the triangular broken pediment on the central section with a proper classical cornice build-up: a projecting horizontal entablature with bed molding, fascia board, and crown molding — no pediment, no triangular gable, no finial. The cornice should project 80–120 mm forward of the carcass face and run the full width of the central section; Redesign the side section crown moldings as graceful curved profiles (ogee or cyma reversa) — a single curved molding transitioning from the top of the side unit body, subordinate in scale to the central cornice. Remove the dentil courses and pilaster capitals from the side section tops; Add clearly visible curved base moldings to the bottom of each side section — an ogee or cove profile flowing from the body of the side unit toward the plinth, matching the character of the curved crown above; Redesign the lower central drawer bank to show 3 rows × 2 columns = 6 drawer fronts. Each row should be divided vertically into two equal drawer fronts side by side, with bin pulls or cup pulls on each; Ensure all cupboard doors (lower central flanking doors and side wing lower doors) feature clearly visible raised-panel construction with the panel proud of the surrounding stile-and-rail frame; Confirm the counter/bridge shelf finish with the client — if two-tone is approved, the natural wood top is acceptable; otherwise paint to match the carcass in heritage cream; Widen the central section proportionally relative to the side wings to better reflect the 1400–1600 mm central vs. 700–900 mm side wing specification; Consider replacing the Corinthian pilaster capitals with simpler classical pilaster-like stiles (fluted or plain) that read as architectural furniture elements rather than full architectural columns — this will better match the Georgian breakfront typology; Ensure full back panels are visible or implied in the side wing shelving bays — the open-sided appearance should be corrected to show enclosed bays

strengths: Photorealistic render quality is excellent — lighting, shadows, and material textures are convincing and professionally presented
The tripartite breakfront massing is clearly established with a dominant central section flanked by two shorter side wings — the stepped hierarchical roofline reads correctly
Heritage cream/off-white painted finish is well-executed and period-appropriate throughout
The counter/bridge shelf with a contrasting natural wood top is visible and clearly marks the mid-height datum transition between upper and lower zones
The lower central drawer bank is present and correctly positioned between flanking cupboard doors
Upper central glazed door pair is present and correctly positioned between open shelving bays
Open shelving bays on both sides of the upper central section are populated with books and display objects, adding realism
Side wing lower cupboard doors are present and symmetrically placed
The continuous plinth base runs across all three sections, unifying the composition
The formal interior context (hardwood floor, coffered ceiling, period room) is appropriate and enhances the presentation
Antique brass/bronze hardware knobs are visible and period-appropriate
The depth transition at counter level appears to be represented, with the upper central section stepping back
issues: CRITICAL: The central section crown is rendered as a full classical pediment (triangular broken pediment with acanthus finial) rather than a standard Georgian/Victorian classical cornice build-up (bed molding, fascia, crown). A pediment is an overly ornate and architecturally specific element not specified — it reads as a Baroque or Neoclassical secretaire rather than a Georgian breakfront bookcase cornice

CRITICAL: The side section crown moldings appear as heavy horizontal classical entablatures with dentil courses and pilaster capitals — these do not read as the specified curved decorative crown moldings (ogee, cove, cyma recta, or cyma reversa profiles). The side crowns should be graceful curved transitions, not full entablature treatments

CRITICAL: The side section base moldings do not appear to feature the specified curved decorative base molding profiles — the bases appear as simple flat plinths without the ogee/cove/cyma reversa curved profile that is described as a non-negotiable critical detail
The lower central drawer bank shows approximately 4 drawers in a single column rather than the specified 3 rows × 2 columns (approximately 6 drawers total) — the face subdivision is incorrect

The lower central flanking cupboard doors (left and right of the drawer bank) appear as flat recessed-panel doors rather than traditional raised-panel stile-and-rail construction — the panel profile reads as inset/flat rather than raised

The side wing upper shelving appears to have 3 shelves per side, which is acceptable, but the shelving bays on the side wings appear to be open on both sides (no back panel visible), which conflicts with the full structural back panel requirement

The pilaster-like front stiles at module junctions are present but are rendered as full Corinthian pilasters with carved capitals — this is more ornate than the specification's 'pilaster-like stiles' language implies, though it is not strictly incorrect for a high-end Georgian piece

The counter/bridge shelf top surface appears to be a natural wood finish (oak or similar) rather than painted to match the carcass — this may be acceptable as a two-tone scheme but requires client confirmation per the specification

The upper central section appears narrower relative to the side wings than the specification suggests (central section should be approximately 1400–1600 mm vs. side wings at 700–900 mm each) — the proportional width ratio appears compressed

The side wing height differential relative to the central section appears to be approximately correct at 75–80%, but the pediment on the central section artificially inflates the apparent height differential beyond what the carcass alone would show
Drawer pulls on the lower central bank appear as small round knobs rather than the specified bin pulls or cup pulls — while knobs are period-appropriate, bin pulls are the preferred specification
suggestions: Replace the triangular broken pediment on the central section with a proper classical cornice build-up: a projecting horizontal entablature with bed molding, fascia board, and crown molding — no pediment, no triangular gable, no finial. The cornice should project 80–120 mm forward of the carcass face and run the full width of the central section

Redesign the side section crown moldings as graceful curved profiles (ogee or cyma reversa) — a single curved molding transitioning from the top of the side unit body, subordinate in scale to the central cornice. Remove the dentil courses and pilaster capitals from the side section tops

Add clearly visible curved base moldings to the bottom of each side section — an ogee or cove profile flowing from the body of the side unit toward the plinth, matching the character of the curved crown above

Redesign the lower central drawer bank to show 3 rows × 2 columns = 6 drawer fronts. Each row should be divided vertically into two equal drawer fronts side by side, with bin pulls or cup pulls on each
Ensure all cupboard doors (lower central flanking doors and side wing lower doors) feature clearly visible raised-panel construction with the panel proud of the surrounding stile-and-rail frame
Confirm the counter/bridge shelf finish with the client — if two-tone is approved, the natural wood top is acceptable; otherwise paint to match the carcass in heritage cream
Widen the central section proportionally relative to the side wings to better reflect the 1400–1600 mm central vs. 700–900 mm side wing specification

Consider replacing the Corinthian pilaster capitals with simpler classical pilaster-like stiles (fluted or plain) that read as architectural furniture elements rather than full architectural columns — this will better match the Georgian breakfront typology
Ensure full back panels are visible or implied in the side wing shelving bays — the open-sided appearance should be corrected to show enclosed bays

review_result.regeneration_instructions: CRITICAL CORRECTIONS REQUIRED: (1) Remove the triangular broken pediment and finial from the central section crown entirely. Replace with a projecting classical cornice build-up: horizontal bed molding + fascia + crown molding projecting 80–120 mm forward, running the full width of the central section — Georgian breakfront bookcase cornice, NOT a pediment. (2) Redesign the side section top crowns as single graceful curved molding profiles (ogee or cyma reversa) — NOT a full entablature with dentils and pilaster capitals. The side crowns must be curved transitional moldings, subordinate to the central cornice. (3) Add clearly visible curved base moldings (ogee or cove profile) at the bottom of each side section above the plinth. (4) Redesign the lower central drawer bank as 3 rows × 2 columns = 6 drawer fronts with bin pulls or cup pulls in antique brass. (5) Ensure all cupboard doors show traditional RAISED-panel construction with the panel visibly proud of the frame. (6) Widen the central section to approximately 1400–1600 mm vs. side wings at 700–900 mm each for correct Palladian proportions. (7) Use simpler fluted or plain pilaster-like stiles at module junctions rather than full Corinthian column capitals. (8) Ensure all shelving bays have visible full back panels — no open-sided bays.

Include wall anchor bracket positions marked in plan at the correct locations (minimum 2 per section, 6 total)
Show the plinth/toe-kick recess (60–80 mm setback) as a plan line at the front face
If a separate elevation is also generated, ensure the stepped roofline (central tallest, sides at 75–80% height) is clearly shown with height dimension annotations

review_result.regeneration_instructions: FLOOR PLAN REQUIREMENTS — CRITICAL CORRECTIONS NEEDED: Generate a true top-down orthographic floor plan (plan view, not elevation). The view must show: (1) A horizontal wall section cut at the top of the drawing with hatching indicating masonry or stud wall. (2) The full depth footprint of the unit flush against the wall — lower base cabinet depth 450–500 mm shown as a measurable plan dimension; upper central section stepped back to 300–350 mm depth shown as a second inner plan line creating a visible counter ledge of 100–150 mm. (3) Three clearly differentiated sections in plan: central section approximately 1400–1600 mm wide (widest), left side wing approximately 700–900 mm wide, right side wing approximately 700–900 mm wide — strict bilateral symmetry. (4) Door swing arcs for all cupboard doors: two lower central flanking doors, two side wing lower doors — shown as quarter-circle arcs opening outward. (5) Drawer bank location in lower central section indicated by dashed rectangle with internal division lines showing 2-column layout. (6) Vertical pilaster stiles shown as thickened lines at all module junctions and outer edges. (7) Plinth/toe-kick recess shown as a setback line 60–80 mm from the front face. (8) Wall-fixing anchor bracket positions marked with standard anchor symbols at minimum 2 per section (6 total), located at the rear of each section against the wall. (9) Correct dimension strings: overall width 3000–3400 mm, central section width, each side wing width, lower base depth 450–500 mm, upper central section depth 300–350 mm, counter ledge depth 100–150 mm. (10) DO NOT show a front elevation — this must be a plan view only. DO NOT use '16000' as a dimension — the correct overall width is 3000–3400 mm. Line weights: wall cut = heaviest, carcass outline = medium-heavy, internal elements = medium, dimension lines = thin.
